validation.codeInvalid: Room codes are 5 characters: A-Z and 2-9 (no O, 0, I or 1).

presets.heading: Saved setups
presets.empty: No saved setups yet — configure options below, name it, and Save.

random.name: Random
random.description: Terrain, number tokens and harbors are shuffled for every game.

beginner.name: Beginner
beginner.description: The official fixed starter board from the rulebook — the same layout every game (base board only).

fiveSixNewPlayers.name: 5-6 New Players
fiveSixNewPlayers.description: The official fixed 5-6 starter board. Coming soon.

headingForNewShores.name: Maiden Voyage
headingForNewShores.description: Open Seas intro scenario: main island plus outer isles. First to 14 VP wins.

newWorld.name: Open Horizons
newWorld.description: 5-6 players only: a randomly assembled main island plus outer isles, sized for a bigger crew. First to 14 VP wins.

throughTheDesert.name: The Great Divide
throughTheDesert.description: 5-6 players only: one main island crossed by a band of desert, plus outer isles beyond. First to 14 VP wins.

forgottenTribe.name: The Lost Isles
forgottenTribe.description: 5-6 players only: one main island surrounded by several small islands, each holding a reward for its first settler. First to 14 VP wins.

sixIslands.name: The Six Islands
sixIslands.description: 5-6 players only: no home island at all — six equal-sized islands to settle instead. First to 18 VP wins.

fogIslands.name: The Misted Isles
fogIslands.description: 5-6 players only: much of the map starts hidden. Sail your ships to reveal facedown terrain and number tiles as you explore.

clothForHexhaven.name: Cloth for Hexhaven
clothForHexhaven.description: 5-6 players only: small-island villages weave cloth when their number rolls. Every 2 cloth is worth 1 victory point.

pirateIslands.name: Corsair Coves
pirateIslands.description: 5-6 players only: the pirate ship patrols a fixed route every turn, blocking nearby ships. Raid its lairs on the small islands for bonus victory points.

wondersOfHexhaven.name: The Wonders of Hexhaven
wondersOfHexhaven.description: 5-6 players only: keep building settlements and cities to raise a wonder in stages. Complete every stage for an instant win, or race to the usual victory point target.

size.seafarers: Open Seas scenario board with sea, ships and the pirate.

fiveSix.name: 5-6 Player Extension
fiveSix.description: Play with up to six around the table.

tradersBarbarians.name: Roads & Raiders
tradersBarbarians.description: Five standalone scenarios: fish, rivers, camel caravans, a barbarian invasion, or trade wagons.

explorersPirates.name: Sails & Scoundrels
explorersPirates.description: Sail beyond your home island through the fog, found new settlements, and build harbor settlements instead of cities.

landHo.name: Landfall
landHo.description: The intro scenario: move ships, explore the fog, and found settlements/harbor settlements. First to 8 VP wins.

fishForHexhaven.name: Fish for Hexhaven
fishForHexhaven.description: Landfall's voyage, plus fish shoals in the fog: haul fish on arrival and deliver it to the council for bonus VP. First to 10 VP wins.

spicesForHexhaven.name: Spices for Hexhaven
spicesForHexhaven.description: Landfall's voyage, plus trading villages: trade for spice at a village, then deliver it to the council for bonus VP and wider ship range. First to 10 VP wins.

pirateLairs.name: The Pirate Lairs
pirateLairs.description: Landfall's voyage, plus pirate lairs in the fog: build crews, land them on a lair, and capture it with your fellow explorers for bonus VP. First to 10 VP wins.

fullCampaign.name: The Sails & Scoundrels
fullCampaign.description: The full campaign: Landfall's voyage with fish, spices, and pirate lairs all in play together. First to 17 VP wins.

fishermen.name: The Fishermen
fishermen.description: A Lake and fishing grounds hand out fish tokens — trade them for one-shot benefits. Watch out for the Old Boot.

rivers.name: The Rivers
rivers.description: Rivers cross the island. Settling or bridging their banks earns gold coins — the Wealthiest and Poorest Settler score bonus/penalty VP.

caravans.name: The Caravans
caravans.description: The desert becomes an Oasis. Every new settlement opens a bidding vote for where the next camel goes. Play to 12 VP.

barbarianAttack.name: Barbarian Attack
barbarianAttack.description: Barbarians march on the island every roll. Recruit and move knights to intercept them before they pillage.

tradersBarbarians.name: Roads & Raiders
tradersBarbarians.description: Three trade hexes turn commodities into gold. Build a wagon with every city and route it past barbarians to deliver.

winnability.warning: This setup may be unwinnable — the highest reachable score is ~{{max}} VP. Lower the VP target or raise the limits.

customTargetVp.name: Custom target VP
customTargetVp.description: Change the number of victory points needed to win.

combine2sAnd12s.name: Combine 2s & 12s
combine2sAnd12s.description: A roll of 2 also produces the 12 hexes, and a roll of 12 also produces the 2 hexes.

eventCards.name: Event Cards
eventCards.description: Replace the two dice with a shuffled 36-card event deck matching the dice odds, without the streaks.

friendlyRobber.name: Friendly Robber
friendlyRobber.description: The robber can't steal from a player at 2 victory points or fewer, and a 7 rolled in round 1 moves nobody at all.

playDevSameTurn.name: Play dev card same turn
playDevSameTurn.description: Development cards may be played the same turn they were bought.

harbormaster.name: Harbormaster
harbormaster.description: The player with the most harbor buildings (settlement 1, city 2; minimum 3) holds a +2 victory point card, stealable like Longest Road.

cardMods.name: Card Mods
cardMods.description: 6 curated new development cards mixed into the deck, plus 5 combined card plays (e.g. Knight + Road Building at once).

helpers.name: The Helpers of Hexhaven
helpers.description: Each player holds one Helper card — a special ability usable at most once per turn-rotation.

hexPieces.name: Multi-piece hex framework
hexPieces.description: Extra robber-like pieces sit on hexes alongside the robber. Pick which ones are in play below. On a 7, the mover may move the robber OR any active piece.
hexPieces.robberNote: The robber is always in play — pick any extra movable pieces below, or none.

wizard.name: Wizard
wizard.description: Every settlement/city adjacent to its hex gets +1 resource when that hex's number is rolled.

trader.name: Trader
trader.description: Acts as a 3:1 bank port for any settlement/city adjacent to its hex, even opponents'. The mover draws 1 resource each time it's moved.

robinHood.name: Robin Hood
robinHood.description: Moving it steals 1 resource card from the wealthiest player and gives it to the poorest.

banker.name: Banker
banker.description: Produces its hex's resource for whoever last moved it there, each time that hex's number is rolled.

poaching.name: Poaching
poaching.description: The mover draws 1 of whatever resource the destination hex produces, each time it's moved.

customConstants.name: Custom game
customConstants.description: Tune production yield, Road Building/Year of Plenty counts, starting resources, the discard limit, build costs and bank size.
customConstants.startingResourcesHint: Each 0–{{max}} (limited by the bank ÷ player count).

fields.maxProgressCards: Max progress cards in hand (Walls & Watchmen)

shuffleNumbers.name: Shuffle numbers
shuffleNumbers.description: Randomize which hex gets which number, keeping the same count of each number (still no two 6s or 8s side by side).

hiddenSetupNumbers.name: Blind placement
hiddenSetupNumbers.description: Hide every hex's number until all starting settlements are placed, then reveal them — place your first settlements without knowing the odds.

incompatibleReasons.eventCardsVsCitiesKnights: Not compatible with Walls & Watchmen's event die.

sbp.name: Special Building Phase
sbp.description: 2015 rule: between turns, every other player may build or buy dev cards in clockwise order — no trading, no dev-card plays, no winning.

pairedPlayers.name: Paired Players
pairedPlayers.description: 2022 rule: a second "player 2" takes a partial turn after player 1 — supply trades, building and one dev card (and may even win).

room.heading: Lobby
room.summaryUnavailable: Full options aren't visible to joined players yet.
room.startHint: All seats must be filled and ready before the game can start.
